fields.description:
## Why
Audit Reliability finding #1 (HIGH). Two clones can both claim the same open ticket (the write lock is per-clone). On merge the STATUS fork resolves deterministically (lower event UUID wins, `reducer/_processors.py` ~L145) and `assignee` resolves by HLC last-writer-wins — but **both agents were told CLAIMED** and proceed to duplicate work. The resolved fork is only a replay-time `logger.warning("PARENT_CHAIN_FORK_RESOLVED …")` (`reducer/_processors.py` ~L159-167), surfaced by no user-facing command. There is no two-clone concurrent-claim test (audit 7.1): `test_store_concurrency.py::test_claim_storm_one_winner` is single-clone.

## Design note (durable + best-effort split)
A lost claim is only observable to a clone once the two clones' event logs have MERGED locally. `push.push_after_commit` is best-effort and may not merge (`sync.push=off`/`async`, unreachable remote). So a successful `claim` keeps its EXISTING contract ("claimed locally; pending cross-clone convergence") — this ticket does NOT reinterpret success as "you won". Detection is split: (a) a GUARANTEED durable record in pure derived state, surfaced by fsck/show whenever the merge lands; (b) a BEST-EFFORT exit-10 that fires only when the local (already-merged) log shows this clone lost — never a false "you won".

## What / files
1. **Pure derived-state fork record** — `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py`: at the fork-resolution branch (right after the `PARENT_CHAIN_FORK_RESOLVED` warning), append to the ticket's derived state: `state.setdefault("status_fork_resolutions", []).append({"winner_uuid", "dropped_uuid", "loser_env_id"})`. Pure/idempotent — rebuilt identically on every full replay; NO alert_store, NO repo_root, NO external I/O, NO cross-subsystem import.
2. **fsck finding** — `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py`: read `status_fork_resolutions` from the reduced tickets and emit, via the existing `lines.append("<TAG>: …")` + `issue_count` pattern, `STATUS_FORK_RESOLVED: <ticket> — concurrent claim/status race resolved (dropped uuid=<…>, loser env=<…>)`.
3. **show surfacing** — because `show` renders the full reduced ticket state as JSON, the new `status_fork_resolutions` field is surfaced automatically in `show <id> --output json`; no separate formatter code is required. (No bespoke text-annotation format is added — the JSON field IS the surfaced annotation.)
4. **Best-effort claim-loss signal** — `src/rebar/_commands/claim.py` `claim_compute`, after `push.push_after_commit(tracker)`: re-read the reduced ticket from the LOCAL store; if it now carries a `status_fork_resolutions` entry whose `loser_env_id` equals THIS clone's env_id (our claim lost the resolved fork), return the ConcurrencyMismatch signal (**exit 10**) with a "claim lost on cross-clone merge" message. Otherwise return success unchanged.
5. **docs** — `docs/concurrency.md`: a short note under the fork-resolution invariant (I8) documenting the derived record → fsck/show + best-effort exit-10.
6. **Tests** (two distinct tests):
   - **(a) reducer replay-idempotency unit test** — `tests/unit/test_reducer_status_fork_record.py` (new): build an event log with TWO concurrent forking STATUS events for one ticket; reduce it, assert the reduced ticket has exactly one `status_fork_resolutions` entry with the correct winner/dropped/loser_env; reduce the SAME log a second time and assert the field is byte-identical (proving the record is pure/idempotent, not a growing side effect).
   - **(b) two-clone integration test** — `tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py` using `two_clones`: both clones claim the same open ticket offline, then push/merge BOTH ways so each local log holds the UNIONED events. Assert: converged `assignee` is deterministic and identical across clones; the losing clone's reduced ticket carries the fork record and `fsck` emits `STATUS_FORK_RESOLVED`; and the exit-10 loss path is EXERCISED — the losing clone's claim-loss detection against the merged log returns exit 10 (assert the exit code / ConcurrencyMismatch explicitly).

## Scope
In: `reducer/_processors.py`, `_commands/fsck.py`, `_commands/claim.py`, `docs/concurrency.md`, the two tests. Out: changing the fork-resolution rule (lower-UUID-wins stays, I8); a bespoke text annotation for `show` beyond the JSON field; distributed locking; forcing a synchronous fetch/merge into the claim path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Resolved STATUS forks are recorded in PURE derived state by the reducer (no I/O/alert_store/repo_root), asserted by the reducer replay-idempotency unit test in `tests/unit/test_reducer_status_fork_record.py` (record correct AND byte-identical across two replays)
- [ ] `fsck` emits a `STATUS_FORK_RESOLVED` finding from that derived state, and `show <id> --output json` includes the `status_fork_resolutions` field (asserted in the two-clone test)
- [ ] The best-effort claim-loss path returns exit 10 when the LOCAL merged log shows this clone is the fork loser, and success otherwise; the two-clone integration test EXERCISES the exit-10 path explicitly (asserts exit 10 / ConcurrencyMismatch)
- [ ] The two-clone regression test asserts a deterministic converged assignee identical across both clones + the fork surfaced
- [ ] `docs/concurrency.md` documents the surfacing behaviour; `make test` green
